items.claim: Conor McGregor has a 2-1 record at welterweight.
Conor McGregor is coming off a five-year layoff.
items.quote: it's been five years this show started during co so that was 2021 I mean it was it was our early days crazy crazy times but what I can tell you is we're live every single Thursday

items.claim: Max Holloway has fought a higher level of competition than Conor McGregor recently.
items.quote: Max Holloway is that he's fought the absolute best of the best in terms of competition.

items.claim: Max Holloway's move to welterweight will benefit his brain health and recovery compared to his previous weight classes.
items.quote: It's extra 25 pounds for his brain to feel good, to heal, to to not get knocked out.

items.claim: Conor McGregor lacks the cardio to sustain a high pace over 25 minutes.
items.quote: Conor McGregor even in his prime, his premier state, guys. Did he have good cardio? I don't know.

items.claim: Conor McGregor did not spend his five-year layoff training and living like an athlete.
items.quote: those five years weren't spent training and growing as an athlete. He wasn't living like an athlete.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's performance in his second fight against Dustin Poirier showed that his decline began over five years ago.
items.quote: if you watch that second Porier fight, you're like, "Oh my god, it was over five and a half years ago."

items.claim: Max Holloway possesses elite cardio and high striking volume.
Max Holloway is incredibly durable despite the damage he has taken throughout his career.
items.quote: Max obviously known for being incredibly durable. I know he's he's taken a lot of damage at this point.
